output: Completed T2 verification. TypeScript compilation passed, the full `src/tools/video-editor` Vitest module passed, and the throwaway threshold-reproduction script confirmed the expected landscape/portrait/floor behavior before being deleted. Best-effort checkpointing to `.megaplan/plans/make-the-video-editor-switch-20260406-0741/execution_batch_2.json` was not possible because the file was not writable.
deviations: Checkpoint file `.megaplan/plans/make-the-video-editor-switch-20260406-0741/execution_batch_2.json` was not writable, so no batch checkpoint was written.

`npx tsx verify-condensed-threshold.ts` failed in the sandbox with `EPERM` on its IPC pipe; the same script was then run successfully with `node --import tsx/esm verify-condensed-threshold.ts` and deleted afterward.
Advisory: done tasks rely on commands_run without files_changed (FLAG-006 softening): T2
Advisory audit finding: Git status shows changed files not claimed by any task: .megaplan/debt.json, .megaplan/plans/make-the-video-editor-switch-20260406-0741

task_updates.executor_notes: Verification passed. `npx tsc --noEmit` exited 0. The full video-editor test module passed with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor` (`32` test files, `127` tests, `0` failures). A throwaway script importing `parseResolution` reproduced the threshold math and confirmed: `1920x1080 @ 1200px -> availablePreviewWidth 804, minPreviewHeight 452.25, threshold 684.25`; `1080x1920 @ 1200px -> availablePreviewWidth 804, minPreviewHeight 1429.33, threshold 1661.33`; `1920x1080 @ 500px -> availablePreviewWidth 104, minPreviewHeight 180, threshold 412`; `<396px width -> availablePreviewWidth 0, minPreviewHeight 180, threshold 412`. The script also asserted that portrait threshold exceeds landscape threshold and that the `MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT=180` floor is never violated. Initial execution via `npx tsx` hit a sandbox IPC restriction, so it was rerun successfully with `node --import tsx/esm`; the script was then deleted.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors, the full `src/tools/video-editor` test module passed, and the reproduced math showed the expected behavior: portrait threshold (`1661.33`) is far larger than landscape (`684.25`) at the same width, while narrow widths clamp to the `180px` preview floor and produce a `412px` threshold.
